noLighthouseData: No Lighthouse data available. Run an Instant Pages scan to get detailed performance metrics.
noCwvData: No Core Web Vitals data available. Run an Instant Pages scan to get performance metrics.
noSitemapData: No sitemap data available. Run a full site scan to fetch sitemap.
noRobotsTxtData: No robots.txt data available. Run a full site scan to fetch.
noIssuesFoundDescription: Your website has no detected SEO issues!
issueHighSimilarityDesc: Multiple elements with very similar content
issueTooManyElementsDesc: Page has excessive number of DOM elements
sectionTechnicalDesc: Server response, crawlability, and site infrastructure
sectionOnPageDesc: Titles, meta descriptions, headings, and HTML tags
sectionContentDesc: Content quality, depth, uniqueness, and readability
sectionLinksDesc: Internal linking, broken links, and link structure
sectionImagesDesc: Alt text, image size, and optimization
sectionStructuredDataDesc: Schema.org markup and rich snippet eligibility
scanStarted: SEO audit scan started. This typically takes 1-5 minutes.
scanStartedDescription: The page will update automatically when the scan completes.
noScanDataDescription: Run your first on-site SEO audit to analyze technical health, page performance, and identify optimization opportunities.
scanTypicalTime: Scan typically takes 1-5 minutes depending on site size
scanDescQueued: Starting scan — queuing SEO audit and site crawl...
scanDescBothRunning: Two parallel jobs running: SEO audit (homepage checks) and site crawl (discovering all pages)...
scanDescAuditDoneCrawling: SEO audit finished. Site crawl still discovering and indexing pages — this takes 2-5 minutes...
scanDescCrawlDoneAuditing: Site crawl finished. SEO audit still processing...
scanDescProcessing: Both jobs done. Running post-crawl analytics (links, images, redirects, word frequency, robots)...
auditStarting: Starting full site audit (use_sitemap, max 100 pages)...
auditQueued: Queued, waiting for sitemap/robots fetch to finish
auditErrorOccurred: An error occurred during the audit
crawlRunning: Crawling site (depth 2, max 100 pages)... {pages} pages found
crawlQueued: Queued, will start after audit kicks off
noPagesFailingCheck: No pages are currently failing the {title} check.
noPageDataDescription: Run a full site scan to see page-level SEO audit results.
clickRowForDetails: click a row for full details
pageSpeedQueuedWaiting: Job queued, waiting for Lighthouse to start analyzing pages...
noPageSpeedDataDescription: Run Lighthouse analysis on your crawled pages to see detailed performance scores and Core Web Vitals for each URL.
quickWinsDescription: Keywords in striking distance — low difficulty, decent volume, positions 4-30
noQuickWinsDescription: No keywords in striking distance yet. Track more keywords to discover quick win opportunities.
addCompetitorsAndRunBacklinks: Add competitors and run backlink analysis to see link building targets.
title: Title
serpNotFetchedYet: SERP data hasn't been fetched yet. Run a SERP check to see competitors.
addKeywordToMonitoring: Add this keyword to monitoring to see SERP competitor data.
recCloseRefDomainGapTitle: Close the referring domain gap (+{gap} gap)
recCloseRefDomainGapDesc: Competitors have on average {avgComp} referring domains vs your {yours}. Focus on acquiring links from domains that already link to competitors.
recCloseRefDomainGapStep1: Target the top {topCount} gap domains that link to {compCount}+ competitors
recCloseRefDomainGapStep2: Start with guest posting or resource link outreach to highest priority targets
recCloseRefDomainGapStep3: Focus on dofollow links from domains with rank 50+
recCloseRefDomainGapStep4: Track new referring domains weekly to measure progress
recSharedLinkSourcesDesc: These domains link to multiple competitors but not to you — they are likely open to linking to similar content.
recSharedLinkSourcesStep2: Analyze what content competitors used to earn these links
recSharedLinkSourcesStep3: Create equal or better content to pitch for link placement
recInformationalDesc: Searchers want detailed answers. Structure content for comprehensive coverage with clear headings.
recInformationalStep1: Add an FAQ section with related questions
recInformationalStep2: Include data, statistics, and expert quotes
recInformationalStep3: Add table of contents for long content
recInformationalStep4: Use schema markup (FAQ, HowTo) for rich results
recCommercialDesc: Searchers are comparing options. Create content that helps with decision-making.
recCommercialStep1: Add comparison tables with clear feature breakdowns
recCommercialStep2: Include pros/cons lists and user reviews
recCommercialStep3: Add pricing information and value propositions
recCommercialStep4: Use Product schema markup for rich results
recTransactionalDesc: Searchers are ready to act. Minimize friction between landing and conversion.
recTransactionalStep1: Add clear CTAs above the fold
recTransactionalStep2: Include trust signals: reviews, certifications, guarantees
recTransactionalStep3: Optimize page speed — every second counts for conversions
recFeaturedSnippetDesc: This keyword shows a featured snippet or PAA boxes — winning these can jump you to position 0.
recFeaturedSnippetStep1: Add a concise 40-60 word answer to the main question near the top
recFeaturedSnippetStep2: Use a paragraph, list, or table format matching the current snippet type
recFeaturedSnippetStep3: Add H2/H3 headers phrased as common questions
recLocalPackDesc: Google shows a local map pack. If you have a physical location, optimize for local SEO.
recLocalPackStep1: Claim and optimize your Google Business Profile
recLocalPackStep2: Ensure NAP consistency (Name, Address, Phone) across all directories
recLocalPackStep3: Get local reviews on Google and industry-specific platforms
recVideoDesc: Google displays video results for this keyword. Creating video content could earn you an additional SERP spot.
recVideoStep1: Create a YouTube video targeting this keyword
recVideoStep2: Embed the video on your ranking page
recPage1OptDesc: You're at position #{position}. Small technical improvements can push you into top 3.
recPage1OptStep1: Improve page Core Web Vitals (LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1)
recPage1OptStep2: Optimize title tag and meta description for higher CTR
recPage1OptStep3: Add internal links from your highest-authority pages
recPage1OptStep4: Ensure content is more comprehensive than top 3 results
recPage2PushDesc: Position #{position} is close to page 1. Focus on both content depth and link authority.
recPage2PushStep1: Expand content by 30-50% with additional subtopics
recPage2PushStep2: Build 5-10 quality backlinks to this page specifically
recPage2PushStep3: Add 3-5 internal links from relevant pages
recPage2PushStep4: Improve page load speed and mobile usability
scoringOverviewDesc: Averaged scoring across all analyzed pages based on a 4-axis algorithm aligned with Google 2026 ranking factors.
radarDesc: Average score per axis across all pages
axisDetailTechnical: Server response, crawlability, SSL, page speed, and infrastructure health.
axisDetailContent: Content depth, readability, heading structure, meta quality, and keyword relevance.
axisDetailSeoPerformance: Lighthouse scores, Core Web Vitals, keyword rankings, and SERP visibility.
axisDetailStrategic: Backlink authority, internal linking, search intent alignment, and competitive positioning.
noScoringDataDesc: Run a full site scan and page scoring to see the 4-axis breakdown.
